roadmap.milestone_count_rationale: 3 milestones: Given Ritika's budget of 179.2 hours and the comprehensive skill gap from student to Junior Frontend Developer, 3 identity-transforming milestones provide a balanced progression without exceeding the available time, ensuring achievable steps for a beginner with low confidence. This count also fits within the calculated demand of 157.5 hours.

vision_profile.current_state: A final-year B.Sc Math student with basic computer skills, eager to start a career in tech.
vision_profile.main_blocker: Lack of foundational programming and web development skills, coupled with low confidence and fear of competition from peers with more resources.
vision_profile.top_motivation: To achieve financial independence quickly to support her family before her younger brother starts college.

milestones.label: M01
milestones.o: After this milestone, Ritika can write basic, structured code in JavaScript, create simple static web pages with HTML, and style them using CSS, demonstrating foundational web development principles.
milestones.module_count_rationale: 4 modules are chosen to cover the breadth of foundational programming logic, structured content, basic styling, and an introduction to software quality, which are essential for any web developer.
milestones.identity_statement: You are building the bedrock of your programming journey, transforming from a student into a logical thinker who can instruct computers to create web experiences.

project.title: Personal Static Portfolio Page
project.description: Build a simple, multi-page static portfolio website using HTML and CSS to showcase your basic information and skills. The site should be responsive for mobile viewing.
project.deliverable: A deployed static website URL and its source code repository.
seeded_errors: A CSS rule intended for mobile layout breaks on larger screens due to incorrect media query application.
An HTML element is improperly nested, causing layout issues and accessibility warnings.

modules.title: JavaScript Fundamentals & Logic
modules.skill_count_rationale: 5 skills are necessary to cover core programming concepts, data types, control flow, and functions, which are the building blocks for any interactive web application.

skills.title: JavaScript Variables & Data Types
skills.why_this_skill: Understanding variables and data types is fundamental for storing and manipulating information in any programming language, crucial for dynamic web content.

skills.title: JavaScript Operators & Expressions
skills.why_this_skill: Operators are essential for performing calculations, comparisons, and logical operations, forming the core of any program's decision-making and data manipulation.

skills.title: JavaScript Control Flow
skills.why_this_skill: Control flow statements dictate the order in which instructions are executed, enabling programs to make decisions and repeat actions, crucial for dynamic user interfaces.

skills.title: JavaScript Functions
skills.why_this_skill: Functions are reusable blocks of code that perform specific tasks, promoting modularity and reducing redundancy, vital for building maintainable web applications.

skills.title: JavaScript Error Handling & Debugging
skills.why_this_skill: Effective error handling and debugging are critical for identifying and fixing issues in web applications, ensuring a smooth user experience and robust code.

scenario.title: Identifying Runtime Errors in a Script

modules.title: Structured Web Content (HTML)
modules.skill_count_rationale: 5 skills are needed to thoroughly cover the fundamentals of HTML, from basic document structure to semantic elements and form creation, enabling the learner to build meaningful web pages.

skills.title: HTML Document Structure
skills.why_this_skill: Understanding the basic structure of an HTML document is the first step to creating any web page, defining its content and metadata.

video.title: HTML Basics: Your First Web Page

skills.title: HTML Text, Images & Links
skills.why_this_skill: These are the core content elements of any web page, enabling the display of information, visual media, and navigation between pages.

skills.title: HTML Semantic Elements
skills.why_this_skill: Using semantic HTML improves accessibility, SEO, and code readability, making web pages more meaningful for both users and search engines.

scenario.title: Improving Page Structure for Screen Readers

skills.title: HTML Forms & Input Elements
skills.why_this_skill: Forms are crucial for user interaction, allowing data submission, login, and feedback, making them a cornerstone of dynamic web applications.

skills.title: HTML Accessibility Basics
skills.why_this_skill: Ensuring web pages are accessible to all users, including those with disabilities, is a critical ethical and legal requirement for modern web development.

science.desc: A client reports that their website's navigation menu is unusable for visually impaired users. You need to identify the accessibility issues in the HTML structure and propose ARIA attribute fixes.

modules.title: Styling Web Content (CSS)
modules.skill_count_rationale: 5 skills are essential to cover the core aspects of CSS, from basic styling and selectors to advanced layout techniques and responsive design, enabling the creation of visually appealing and adaptable web interfaces.

skills.title: CSS Selectors & Properties
skills.why_this_skill: Selectors are how you target HTML elements to apply styles, and properties define those styles, forming the foundation of all visual design on the web.

video.title: CSS Fundamentals: Styling Your First Element

skills.title: CSS Layout with Flexbox
skills.why_this_skill: Flexbox is a powerful and widely used CSS module for arranging items in one dimension, making it indispensable for creating flexible and responsive layouts.

skills.title: CSS Layout with Grid
skills.why_this_skill: CSS Grid is the most powerful layout system for two-dimensional layouts, enabling complex web designs with ease and precision, especially for entire page structures.

video.title: Building Complex Layouts with CSS Grid

skills.title: CSS Responsive Design
skills.why_this_skill: Responsive design ensures websites look and function well across all devices and screen sizes, which is critical for reaching a broad audience and providing a consistent user experience.

skills.title: CSS Animations & Transitions
skills.why_this_skill: Adding animations and transitions enhances user experience by providing visual feedback and making interfaces more engaging and intuitive.

modules.title: Basic Software Quality & Deployment
modules.skill_count_rationale: 5 skills are included to introduce essential practices for ensuring code quality, version control, and basic deployment, which are critical for any professional developer.

skills.title: Git & Version Control Basics
skills.why_this_skill: Git is indispensable for tracking code changes, collaborating with teams, and managing different versions of a project, a core tool for all developers.

skills.title: GitHub Fundamentals
skills.why_this_skill: GitHub is the industry standard platform for hosting Git repositories, enabling collaboration, code review, and project management in open source and professional settings.

scenario.title: Submitting a Pull Request for Review

skills.title: Static Site Deployment
skills.why_this_skill: Deploying a static website is the simplest way to make your web projects live and shareable, a crucial step for showcasing your work to potential employers.

skills.title: Code Linting & Formatting
skills.why_this_skill: Consistent code style improves readability, reduces errors, and facilitates collaboration, making your code professional and easier to maintain.

skills.title: Basic Web Security Practices
skills.why_this_skill: Understanding basic security principles is crucial for preventing common vulnerabilities and building robust, trustworthy web applications.

science.desc: Explain the importance of version control with Git and how you would deploy a simple static website to a hosting service like GitHub Pages.

milestones.label: M02
milestones.o: After this milestone, Ritika can build dynamic web pages that interact with users and external APIs, manage asynchronous operations, and display data effectively, forming the core of a modern web application.
milestones.module_count_rationale: 4 modules are selected to cover the critical aspects of client-side interactivity, data fetching, and advanced JavaScript patterns necessary for building dynamic web applications.
milestones.identity_statement: You are now bringing your web pages to life, transforming static content into interactive experiences that communicate with the world.

project.title: Public API Data Fetcher
project.description: Develop a single-page application that fetches data from a public API (e.g., weather API, joke API) and displays it dynamically. Include features like user input for search/filtering and error handling.
project.deliverable: A deployed web application URL, its source code repository, and a brief documentation of the API integration.
seeded_errors: The application fails to display data when the API returns an error, showing a blank screen instead of a user-friendly message.
Asynchronous API calls are not handled correctly, leading to race conditions or incorrect data display.

modules.title: DOM Manipulation & Event Handling
modules.skill_count_rationale: 5 skills are crucial for enabling dynamic user interfaces by allowing JavaScript to interact with and modify the HTML structure and respond to user actions.

skills.title: DOM Selection & Modification
skills.why_this_skill: Directly manipulating the Document Object Model (DOM) is how JavaScript changes the content, structure, and style of a web page in response to user actions or data.

skills.title: JavaScript Event Listeners
skills.why_this_skill: Event listeners are how web pages respond to user interactions like clicks, key presses, and form submissions, making applications interactive and engaging.

skills.title: Client-Side Form Validation with JS
skills.why_this_skill: Client-side validation provides immediate feedback to users, improving usability and reducing unnecessary server requests, a key part of a good user experience.

skills.title: JavaScript Local Storage
skills.why_this_skill: Local storage allows web applications to store data directly in the user's browser, enabling features like saving user preferences or maintaining session state without server interaction.

skills.title: JavaScript Modules
skills.why_this_skill: Modules allow for organizing code into reusable, encapsulated units, improving maintainability, scalability, and collaboration in larger projects.

scenario.title: Refactoring a Monolithic Script into Modules

modules.title: Asynchronous JavaScript & APIs
modules.skill_count_rationale: 5 skills are critical for understanding how web applications fetch data from external sources and handle operations that don't complete immediately, which is fundamental for dynamic content.

skills.title: Asynchronous JavaScript & Callbacks
skills.why_this_skill: Asynchronous programming is essential for web applications to fetch data, handle user input, and perform long-running tasks without freezing the user interface.

skills.title: JavaScript Promises
skills.why_this_skill: Promises provide a cleaner and more manageable way to handle asynchronous operations compared to callbacks, improving code readability and error handling.

skills.title: JavaScript Async/Await
skills.why_this_skill: Async/await provides an even more readable and synchronous-looking syntax for handling Promises, making complex asynchronous code much easier to write and understand.
lessons: Error Handling with Try-Catch in Async/Await

skills.title: Working with the Fetch API
skills.why_this_skill: The Fetch API is the modern standard for making network requests in the browser, enabling web applications to communicate with servers and retrieve dynamic data.

scenario.title: Displaying Data from a Public API

skills.title: Error Handling in API Calls
skills.why_this_skill: Robust error handling for API calls ensures that your application gracefully manages network issues, server errors, and invalid responses, providing a better user experience.

scenario.title: Implementing a Retry Mechanism for Failed API Calls

science.desc: A user reports that your web application sometimes shows outdated data or fails to load content. You suspect an issue with asynchronous API calls. Diagnose and fix the problem to ensure data consistency and reliability.

modules.title: Modern JavaScript Features
modules.skill_count_rationale: 5 skills are included to cover modern JavaScript syntax and features that improve code readability, conciseness, and efficiency, aligning with current industry practices.

skills.title: ES6+ Features (Destructuring, Spread/Rest)
skills.why_this_skill: Modern JavaScript features like destructuring and spread/rest operators simplify common programming tasks, making code cleaner and more efficient.

skills.title: Advanced Array Methods
skills.why_this_skill: Proficiency with array methods like map, filter, and reduce is essential for efficient data manipulation and transformation, a common task in frontend development.

scenario.title: Filtering and Displaying a List of Products

skills.title: JavaScript Object-Oriented Programming
skills.why_this_skill: Understanding OOP principles in JavaScript allows for building more structured, reusable, and maintainable codebases, especially in larger applications.

scenario.title: Designing a Class for UI Components

skills.title: Functional Programming Concepts in JS
skills.why_this_skill: Functional programming paradigms promote writing predictable, testable, and reusable code, which is highly beneficial for complex frontend applications.

scenario.title: Refactoring a Stateful Component to Pure Functions

skills.title: Common JavaScript Design Patterns
skills.why_this_skill: Learning design patterns provides proven solutions to common software design problems, leading to more robust, scalable, and maintainable applications.

scenario.title: Implementing a Pub/Sub System for UI Events

modules.title: Data Presentation & Visualization
modules.skill_count_rationale: 5 skills are included to cover the essential techniques for effectively presenting and visualizing data on the frontend, enhancing user understanding and engagement.

skills.title: Displaying Tabular Data
skills.why_this_skill: Presenting data clearly in tables is a fundamental requirement for many business applications, ensuring information is organized and easy to read.

skills.title: Displaying Data in Lists & Cards
skills.why_this_skill: Lists and cards are versatile UI patterns for displaying collections of items, commonly used in e-commerce, social feeds, and content aggregators.

skills.title: Basic Charting with Libraries
skills.why_this_skill: Visualizing data with charts makes complex information digestible and actionable, a key skill for dashboards and analytical applications.

scenario.title: Displaying Real-time Stock Data on a Chart

skills.title: Frontend Data Filtering & Sorting UI
skills.why_this_skill: Providing intuitive filtering and sorting options directly on the frontend enhances user control and improves the discoverability of information within large datasets.

skills.title: User Feedback & Loading States UI
skills.why_this_skill: Providing clear feedback and managing loading states improves the perceived performance and usability of web applications, reducing user frustration.

science.desc: Describe how you would build a dynamic product listing page that fetches data from an API, allows users to filter and sort products, and handles loading states and errors gracefully.

milestones.label: M03
milestones.o: After this milestone, Ritika can contribute to a frontend codebase, build small features, understand the development lifecycle, and is ready for an entry-level Junior Frontend Developer role.
milestones.module_count_rationale: 4 modules are designed to consolidate learned skills, introduce professional development practices, and prepare the learner for the job market, including advanced tools and interview readiness.
milestones.identity_statement: You are now a capable Junior Frontend Developer, ready to apply your skills in a professional setting and build impactful web experiences.

project.title: Interactive Task Manager Web App
project.description: Build a full-featured interactive task manager web application. It should allow users to add, edit, delete, and mark tasks as complete. Implement local storage for persistence and a responsive design.
project.deliverable: A deployed web application URL, its well-documented source code repository, and a short presentation demonstrating its features and your design choices.
seeded_errors: Tasks added to the list disappear after a page refresh due to incorrect local storage implementation.
The task editing functionality introduces a bug where all tasks are updated instead of just the selected one.

modules.title: Frontend Build Tools & Ecosystem
modules.skill_count_rationale: 5 skills are included to familiarize the learner with essential tools and concepts in the modern frontend ecosystem, crucial for efficient development and deployment.

skills.title: NPM & Yarn Package Managers
skills.why_this_skill: Package managers are fundamental for managing project dependencies, installing libraries, and running scripts, a daily task for frontend developers.

skills.title: Module Bundlers (Webpack/Vite)
skills.why_this_skill: Bundlers optimize frontend assets for production, improving performance and enabling the use of modern JavaScript features across different browsers.

scenario.title: Setting Up a Basic Build Pipeline

skills.title: Transpilers (Babel)
skills.why_this_skill: Transpilers allow developers to write modern JavaScript code while ensuring compatibility with older browsers, broadening the reach of web applications.

skills.title: CSS Preprocessors (Sass/Less)
skills.why_this_skill: CSS preprocessors extend CSS with programming features, making stylesheets more maintainable, reusable, and scalable for larger projects.

scenario.title: Refactoring a Large CSS File with Sass

skills.title: Frontend Testing Basics
skills.why_this_skill: Writing tests ensures the reliability and correctness of your frontend code, preventing regressions and building confidence in your deployments.

scenario.title: Writing Unit Tests for a JavaScript Utility Function

modules.title: Web Performance & Optimization
modules.skill_count_rationale: 5 skills are included to cover essential techniques for improving the speed and responsiveness of web applications, which directly impacts user experience and SEO.

skills.title: Web Performance Metrics
skills.why_this_skill: Understanding key performance metrics allows developers to measure, monitor, and improve the speed and responsiveness of web applications, crucial for user satisfaction.

scenario.title: Analyzing a Slow-Loading Page with Lighthouse

skills.title: Image Optimization
skills.why_this_skill: Optimizing images is one of the most impactful ways to improve web performance, reducing page load times and bandwidth usage.

scenario.title: Reducing Page Load Time by Optimizing Images

skills.title: Code Splitting & Lazy Loading
skills.why_this_skill: These techniques reduce the initial load time of web applications by only loading necessary code when it's needed, improving perceived performance.

scenario.title: Implementing Lazy Loading for an Image Gallery

skills.title: Browser Caching Strategies
skills.why_this_skill: Effective caching reduces server load and improves page load times for returning users by storing assets locally in the browser.

scenario.title: Making a Web App Work Offline with Service Workers

skills.title: Critical Rendering Path Optimization
skills.why_this_skill: Optimizing the critical rendering path ensures that the most important content of a web page is displayed as quickly as possible, improving user perception of speed.

scenario.title: Improving Initial Page Load Speed for a News Website

science.desc: A client complains that their e-commerce website loads slowly, especially on mobile devices, leading to high bounce rates. You need to identify performance bottlenecks using browser tools and propose optimization strategies.

modules.title: Professional Development & Collaboration
modules.skill_count_rationale: 5 skills are included to equip the learner with essential soft skills and tools for effective collaboration, problem-solving, and continuous learning in a professional development environment.

skills.title: Effective Communication for Developers
skills.why_this_skill: Clear and concise communication is vital for collaborating with teammates, understanding requirements, and explaining technical concepts to non-technical stakeholders.

scenario.title: Explaining a Complex Bug to a Project Manager

skills.title: Advanced Problem-Solving Strategies
skills.why_this_skill: Developing strong problem-solving skills is crucial for tackling complex technical challenges and efficiently debugging issues in real-world projects.

scenario.title: Diagnosing a Performance Regression in a Large Application

skills.title: Agile Development & Scrum Basics
skills.why_this_skill: Understanding Agile methodologies like Scrum is essential for working effectively in most modern software development teams, facilitating iterative and collaborative work.

scenario.title: Participating in a Sprint Planning Meeting

skills.title: Continuous Learning & Tech Trends
skills.why_this_skill: The tech industry evolves rapidly; a commitment to continuous learning ensures developers stay relevant and adapt to new technologies and best practices.

scenario.title: Evaluating a New Frontend Framework for a Project

skills.title: Personal Branding & Networking
skills.why_this_skill: Building a professional brand and network is crucial for career growth, discovering opportunities, and connecting with mentors and peers in the industry.

modules.title: Interview Readiness & Portfolio Polish
modules.skill_count_rationale: 5 skills are included to provide targeted preparation for job applications, technical interviews, and showcasing a polished portfolio, directly addressing the goal of securing an entry-level role.

skills.title: Resume & Cover Letter Crafting
skills.why_this_skill: A well-crafted resume and cover letter are your first impression, crucial for standing out to recruiters and securing interview opportunities.

scenario.title: Customizing a Resume for a Junior Frontend Role

skills.title: Behavioral Interview Preparation
skills.why_this_skill: Behavioral interviews assess soft skills and cultural fit, which are as important as technical skills for securing a job and thriving in a team.

scenario.title: Practicing 'Tell me about a time you failed' question

skills.title: Technical Interview Preparation
skills.why_this_skill: Technical interviews are the primary way companies assess coding proficiency and problem-solving abilities, requiring focused practice and understanding of common questions.

scenario.title: Solving a JavaScript Array Manipulation Problem

skills.title: Portfolio Presentation & Storytelling
skills.why_this_skill: Effectively presenting your projects and the story behind them helps interviewers understand your skills, thought process, and contributions.

scenario.title: Presenting Your Task Manager Project to a Mock Interviewer

skills.title: Effective Job Search Strategies
skills.why_this_skill: Knowing how to effectively search for jobs, apply strategically, and follow up increases your chances of securing an entry-level position.

scenario.title: Crafting a Personalized Job Application Strategy

science.desc: You are in a final round interview for a Junior Frontend Developer role. The interviewer asks you to walk through your most challenging project, explaining the technical decisions and how you overcame obstacles.

capability_gap.reasoning: Gap analysis: identity_distance=0.60; experience_mod=0.30; time_mod=0.85; skill_mod=0.00; gap_score=0.77. Recommend 5 milestone(s), 4 module(s) per milestone, 5 skill(s) per module.
